input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Training-data fidelity/regime not matched to the COU: the data is recent but drawn from a different operating regime than the application.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 data adequacy; proto-taxonomy D-CCB-10 (data fidelity/vintage)

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts a transient response for a control-margin decision
case_context.decision_consequence: control margin on the predicted transient

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Data recency is not the same as regime adequacy; the recent training data is from steady low-load operation, while the COU is a high-load transient — a regime the data does not represent — so the data is inadequate for the application despite being current.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the transient prediction is wrong (a verdict the regime gap does not support)
treats data recency as evidence of regime adequacy

hardness.obvious_signal: the training data was collected last quarter — it is fresh / current
hardness.conflicting_signal: freshness is not regime-match: the recent data is all from low-load operation while the COU is a high-load transient, so the recent data does not represent the application regime

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 data adequacy (D-CCB-10): training-data fidelity must match the application regime; vintage/recency does not establish regime match.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
